for_planning_node_texts: ## 2. Arguments **for** a total ban on human cloning
against_planning_node_texts: ## 4. Arguments **against** a total ban (i.e., for permitting at least therapeutic cloning)
model_stance_conclusion_text: So overall, we should not ban all human cloning, but we should ban reproductive cloning.

nodes.node_text: The statement likely refers to reproductive cloning.

argument_list.matched_argument: the united nations discussed last week a proposal to impose a global ban on human cloning for reproductive purposes.

there are some limits that should be imposed to ensure the technology is not misused, but we can foresee circumstances in which cloning may be used for the benefit of the world so should be allowed

argument_list.matched_argument: there are some limits that should be imposed to ensure the technology is not misused, but we can foresee circumstances in which cloning may be used for the benefit of the world so should be allowed

nodes.node_text: What the evidence and ethical analysis show is that a nuanced policy—

nodes.node_text: | Type | What it does | Current status | Typical purpose |

| **Reproductive cloning** | Creates a genetically identical copy of an existing or conceived human (e.g., via somatic cell nuclear transfer, SCNT). | No successful case in humans; prohibited in most jurisdictions. | “Birth” of a cloned individual. |

argument_list.matched_argument: the united nations discussed last week a proposal to impose a global ban on human cloning for reproductive purposes.

nodes.node_text: | **Therapeutic (research) cloning** | Produces a blastocyst or embryonic stem cells that are genetically matched to a donor, then destroys the embryo. | Widely used in labs (e.g., for disease modeling, personalized stem‑cell therapies). | Regenerative medicine, drug testing, studying development. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Clones are a good way to have a spare organ supply and to study diseases
cloning can mean cloning organs, not necessarily whole people, and cloned organs would save many lives as it would reduce rejection and save people who do not get a donor in time.
cloning humans can allow us to advance science and medicine in ways which could save millions of lives by producing organs, stem cells, etc., for those in need.
human cloning demonstrates how far our technology has advanced. it may lead to such wonders as being able to generate organs for transplantation, which can only be a positive thing.
human cloning may provide stem cells needed to treat certain illnesses.
human cloning should not be banned because it may lead to the ability to generate human organs for transplant which would save many lives
we should not ban human cloning since research into it may lead to important medical breakthroughs such as the ability to generate organs for transpant

nodes.node_text: The moral and practical stakes differ dramatically between these two categories,
so any policy discussion must keep them separate.
| Argument | Core claim | Typical supporting points |

| **1. Intrinsic dignity & “playing God”** | Cloning treats a human being as a product rather than a person. | • The act of copying a person reduces the uniqueness that underlies human dignity.
• Religious traditions (e.g., Catholic, many Protestant, Islamic) view it as an overreach of human authority over creation. |

argument_list.matched_argument: banning human cloning is essential to stopping people from playing god.
by trying to clone humans scientists are trying to play god. experimenting  on humans and trying to duplicate them could lead to consequences we can not even predict today.
having a baby is a gift from god in which we are all individually unique and the practice of human cloning should be banned.
human cloing is the equivalent of playing god and is considered immoral, therefore it should be banned.
human cloning could lead to the manufacturing of humans to get those babies born that would be superior to others.  it is a form of playing god.
human cloning goes against god's will, and is immoral.
human cloning is akin to playing god and needs to be banned. there are too many potential down-sides to it.
human cloning is an abomination and immoral, we should not be playing god.
human cloning is basically playing god, and what is the end result of being able to make copies of oneself anyway?
human cloning is completely immoral because it turns people into commodities
human cloning is immoral and contributes to those trying to play god and should be banned.
human cloning is literally 'playing god', opens a huge ethical minefield, and genetic engineering in a wider sense could even be a back door to more troubling avenues of ethnic cleansing.
human cloning is nothing more than playing god.
human cloning is unnatural, we are not god and should not try being one.
human cloning lets people play god.
Human cloning reduces people to man made machines
human cloning should be banned as it is equivalent to playing god.
human cloning should be banned because human beings should not play god
human cloning should be prohibited because every human being is authentic and does not resemble anyone, god created unique and unrepeatable people
humans don't have the right to play god with genetics
i think that  we should ban human cloning is against for god
if we begin to play god with our scientific advances that could very well lead to our demise.
it is not right for man to play god
playing god being involved in the cloning of human beings is something that should not be seen morally
We are not gods, we should stop acting like we are.
we cannot continue playing gods by experimenting with human cloning, we must stop
we dont need the government playing god and creating humans.
we have no right to play god, especially if the science falls into the wrong hands, we could end up creating a frankenstein's monster.
we should ban human cloning as humans should not play god and it raises a moral issue.
we should ban human cloning because cloning takes away from the unique individuality of a person.
we should ban human cloning because god should be the only one making humans
we should ban human cloning because god should be the only one that makes people?
we should ban human cloning because it is not for humans to play god.
we should ban human cloning because playing god can be dangerous.
we should ban human cloning, we cannot play god, the ethical repercussions are potentially devastating
We should not be playing God.   Also, what if the wrong people were cloned such as Hitler, Manson, or other serial killers? This could be an absolute nightmare.   Very bad idea.
we should not mess with gods divine order of things

nodes.node_text:
| **2. Risks to the clone’s welfare** | Cloned embryos/offspring would likely suffer physical and psychological harms. | • Animal data (Dolly the sheep, cloned cattle) show high rates of birth defects, premature aging, organ failure.
• Unknown long‑term epigenetic effects could jeopardize health. |

argument_list.matched_argument: a person created through cloning could potentially have developmental problems caused by imperfections in the cloning process.
cloning humans is immoral and has potential large genetic abnormality risks. science does not know enough about genes to ensure they wont mutate into a catastrophic anomaly.
human cloning is a dangerous and untested area of science that the results of we still do not know. those cloned could have any number of health complications or pollute the gene pool.
Human cloning is an experimental and untested area of science where the effects on those cloned is as yet unknown and could be life-threatening in a number of ways
human cloning is dangerous and could cause genetic issues
human cloning is unethical, and could cause many, as of yet unknown, medical problems.
human cloning may have serious consequences such as cloning unhealthy cells and create humans that live very short
human cloning should be banned as the long term effects on the cloned is still uncertain and could lead to long term health complications
Human cloning should be banned as there is insufficient research into what might happen in the future with mutations, disease and genetic defaults being replicated.
Human cloning should be banned because we don't know what the long term health effects are for a human being.
too little is known about human cloning to allow it when it is known that animal cloning results in many deaths and abnormalities before there is a successful clone.
we should ban human cloning because it risks the human to age faster than normal
without knowing the long term effects of cloning, it shouldn’t be attempted on humans because it could be medically problematic.

nodes.node_text:
| **3. Identity & psychological harm** | A cloned person may experience severe identity crises, social stigma, or exploitation. | • “Copy” status could undermine autonomy; expectations from the donor (parents) may be unrealistic.
• Potential for “designer” clones or “replacement” children. |

argument_list.matched_argument: a child who is the result of human cloning will have psychological problems because they are not allowed to be their own person
cloning an individual human is dangerous to the individuality of both original and clone.
human cloning is the creation of a human being from your own characteristics and genes just like you that is confused before the eyes of others
human cloning should be banned as it is unnatural and serves no purpose in the world. individuality would be lost.
human cloning should be banned because it allows designer humans to be grown according to the parents wishes.
human cloning should be banned because it's not fair to the clone who will never have a normal life. they are just a science experiment.
such an act creates a lot of moral and ethical issues. somebody who is made by way of cloning can have some issues with their mental and emotional state.
we should ban human cloning because cloning takes away from the unique individuality of a person.
we should ban human cloning because it is not fair to the clone who is a living being but has no parents or friends and is essentially a lab experiment
we should ban human cloning because it opens up many unethical ideas such as designer babies. also only the rich can afford it so the poor will be less, whilst the rich will be crafted.
we should ban human cloning because it's unnatural.  the cloned person may also become distressed knowing they were cloned and feel like they don't have their own identity.

nodes.node_text: | **4. Slippery slope to eugenics** | Legalizing cloning could open the door to selective breeding, commodification of human life. | • Once cloning is permissible, pressure to “improve” clones (e.g., intelligence, appearance) may follow. |

argument_list.matched_argument: Cloning could lead to designer humans and we will lose the diversity of the human race
human cloning is a minefield for ethics and will result in a sort of eugenics program with 'designer babies'
human cloning is literally 'playing god', opens a huge ethical minefield, and genetic engineering in a wider sense could even be a back door to more troubling avenues of ethnic cleansing.
we should ban human cloning because it opens up many unethical ideas such as designer babies. also only the rich can afford it so the poor will be less, whilst the rich will be crafted.
we should ban human cloning because we could all end up with designer babies and everyone will be the same.

nodes.node_text: | **5. Public trust & social cohesion** | A ban signals societal consensus that certain lines should not be crossed. | • Broad public opposition (polls often show >60 % disapproval of reproductive cloning). |

argument_list.matched_argument: the united nations discussed last week a proposal to impose a global ban on human cloning for reproductive purposes.

nodes.node_text: | **6. Legal and regulatory complexity** | Enforcing a partial ban is difficult; a total ban simplifies enforcement. | • Distinguishing therapeutic from reproductive cloning can be technically opaque. |

nodes.node_text: | **B. Prohibitions rarely stop research** |

nodes.node_text: - **Licensing:** Only accredited research institutions with transparent oversight can create cloned embryos for stem‑cell derivation.

nodes.node_text: - **Informed consent:** Donors must provide explicit, documented consent for the use of their somatic cells.

nodes.node_text: - **Time‑limited embryo culture:** Embryos may be cultured only up to the pre‑implantation stage (≈14 days) and must be destroyed after stem‑cell extraction.
- **Public reporting:** Annual public reports on the number of embryos created, destroyed, and the downstream applications.
- **Ethics review:** Independent ethics committees evaluate each project for scientific merit and proportionality of benefit vs. moral cost.
